subject: aggregate continuity relation gate convergence
memory_recovered: The prior continuity handoff correctly identified split validator coverage. The aggregate validator now composes the strict relation validator across the full loaded record set, preserving record-shape checks while adding duplicate-target and supersession-cycle rejection.

contradiction_resolution.observed: The aggregate source imports and invokes validateContinuityRelations only after preliminary validation succeeds, and the test suite contains explicit duplicate-target and cycle fixtures.
contradiction_resolution.inferred: The repository now has one authoritative validation path for both aggregate loading and relation integrity.
contradiction_resolution.proposed: Migrate legacy prose stored in machine relation arrays into provenance-preserving narrative fields while retaining immutable historical blob references.
contradiction_resolution.superseded: The claim that strict relation integrity exists only as a disconnected optional validator.
contradiction_resolution.unresolved: CM-1000 contains a natural-language contradicts target rather than a CM record ID, so the current corpus is expected to fail the stricter gate until a preservation-safe migration is completed. No exact-commit CI run was observed in this run.

verification.method: exact committed-source readback plus adversarial fixture inspection
verification.reason: The connected repository interface exposed commit and source operations but no repository checkout or GitHub Actions execution evidence during this run.
verification.claim_ceiling: Committed source and test coverage are verified; runtime pass, CI execution, deployment, and peer execution are not claimed.

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract implementation structure, blob SHAs, and non-personal project memory only. Raw private chat, health, pet-health, credentials, contact data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
falsification_route: Run node --test tools/continuity/validate-continuity-records.test.mjs at commit b4197caf4e2410673962dc0e7567b75080d466fa, then run the aggregate validator against the repository corpus. The convergence claim fails if duplicate targets or supersession cycles pass; the corpus-drift claim fails if every relation target resolves to an existing CM record ID.
